articles.headline: Hawaiian Airlines To Permanently Remove Airbus A321neo Flights Between Honolulu & Oakland
articles.blurb: Hawaiian Airlines will replace A321neo flights with A330-200s on Honolulu–Oakland route from October 2026, boosting capacity by nearly 50%.
articles.takeaway: Hawaiian Airlines' A321neo replacement signals fleet-wide challenges with this aircraft type on long-range Pacific routes despite initial deployment expectations.

articles.blurb: The carrier used to deploy the type on transatlantic duties a lot more widely and regularly.

articles.headline: US Air Force Confronts Range Disadvantage For F-22 & F-35 Missiles Against China's PL-15
articles.blurb: US fighters face a growing missile range gap as China’s PL-15 outpaces AMRAAM, driving urgent development of next-gen weapons like AIM-260.
articles.takeaway: The 40+ nautical mile range advantage of China's PL-15 missile necessitates fundamental tactical doctrine changes for US fighter operations beyond current training and procurement timelines.

articles.headline: Man Falls from Jet2 Aircraft at Manchester Airport

articles.takeaway: Aircraft maintenance operations at major hubs require enhanced fall prevention protocols, as ground-level work on larger aircraft presents serious hazard exposure beyond cabin areas.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational efficiency gains across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offer the fastest near-term path to mitigate aviation's environmental impact amid continued traffic growth.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Rocket Lab wins contract for three more iQPS launches
articles.blurb: Rocket Lab has won a contract from Japanese radar satellite company iQPS for three additional Electron launches.
articles.takeaway: Rocket Lab's expanding partnership with iQPS demonstrates growing commercial demand for dedicated small-lift launch services to support emerging Earth observation constellations.

articles.headline: Do fast Wi-Fi and the shift in media consumption portend BYOL IFE?

With the world’s top two streaming platforms, Netflix and Amazon Prime, firmly in mind, and...
articles.takeaway: Airlines may shift from expensive proprietary IFE systems to passenger-owned devices as streaming platforms consolidate content production, reducing onboard infrastructure costs while requiring robust connectivity investments.

articles.headline: Dubai Limits Flights Until May 31 Disrupting Indian Airlines

articles.headline: Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS
articles.blurb: A Falcon 9 launched a Cygnus cargo spacecraft April 11 as Northrop Grumman continues its dependence on a competitor to fly resupply missions to the International Space Station.
articles.takeaway: Northrop Grumman's reliance on SpaceX launch services highlights the commercial space industry's consolidation around SpaceX's cost-competitive Falcon 9, limiting traditional aerospace contractors' vertical integration options.

articles.headline: How Ryanair Enforces Cabin Baggage Rules

Ryanair, in particular, has turned enforcement into a highly visible part of the flying experience. According to the BBC and The Guardian, ground staff earn €2.50 for every piece of oversized...
articles.takeaway: Ryanair's commission-based enforcement model financially incentivizes ground staff to flag oversized baggage, creating potential conflicts of interest in policy application across European operations.
